ocr.text: The data from Table 1 presents serum magnesium levels with the reference Kurzal [26] showing a mean of 1.60 ± 0.46 mg/dL
and a P value of <0.0005, while Pushpo and Jagdish [27] report a mean of 1.67 ± 0.23 mg/dL with a P value of <0.001.
Smolarczyk et al. [29] indicate a mean of 1.64 ± 0.07 mg/dL with a P value of <0.003, and Wójcicka-Jagodzińska et al. [30]
show a mean of 1.63 ± 0.053 mg/dL with a P value of <0.001. Begum et al. [28] have a mean of 1.77 ± 0.36 mg/dL and a P
value of <0.001, while Shahid et al. [14] report a mean of 1.87 ± 0.3 mg/dL with a P value of <0.001. The present study
indicates a mean serum magnesium level of 1.73 ± 0.4 mg/dL with a P value of <0.05. Table 2 details WPAI questions with
absenteeism for 40-64 years of age (n = 1,339) showing a rate ratio of 0.707 (0.614, 0.815) and P value < 0.0001, while for ≥ 65
years of age (n = 515), the rate ratio is 0.376 (0.289, 0.489) with P value < 0.0001. Presenteeism rates are 0.733 (0.698, 0.769)
with P value < 0.0001 for 40-64 years and 0.406 (0.369, 0.445) with P value < 0.0001 for ≥ 65 years. Overall work impairment
shows a rate ratio of 0.737 (0.702, 0.773) with P value < 0.0001 for 40-64 years and 0.421 (0.384, 0.462) with P value < 0.0001
for ≥ 65 years. Activity impairment reflects a rate ratio of 0.778 (0.745, 0.811) with P value < 0.0001 for 40-64 years and 0.512
(0.472, 0.555) with P value < 0.0001 for ≥ 65 years.
